title: Scaffold dashboard package and dependencies
description: Create the factory/dashboard/ package structure and add all necessary dependencies. Add FastAPI, uvicorn, aiosqlite to pyproject.toml. Create factory/dashboard/__init__.py, factory/dashboard/app.py (FastAPI app with /api/v1 router prefix), factory/dashboard/models.py (empty), factory/dashboard/db.py (empty), factory/dashboard/routers/__init__.py. Add Makefile targets: dashboard (run the dashboard server), dashboard-dev (run with reload). Update .env.example with DASHBOARD_PORT=8420. Create dashboard/frontend/ directory placeholder. Add tests/test_dashboard/ directory with __init__.py.
acceptance_criteria: factory/dashboard/ package exists with __init__.py
factory/dashboard/app.py creates a FastAPI app with APIRouter prefix /api/v1
pyproject.toml includes fastapi, uvicorn[standard], aiosqlite as dependencies
Makefile has 'dashboard' and 'dashboard-dev' targets
.env.example includes DASHBOARD_PORT=8420
dashboard/frontend/ directory exists
tests/test_dashboard/__init__.py exists
make check passes
make test passes

title: Implement event models and SQLite storage layer
description: Define Pydantic models for dashboard events and implement SQLite persistence. In factory/dashboard/models.py: create EventType enum (job_started, job_completed, job_failed, agent_spawned, agent_exited, task_started, task_completed, task_failed, round_result), Event model (id, timestamp, job_id, event_type, agent_role, task_id, details as dict, repo_name), JobSummary model (job_id, repo_name, issue_number, status, started_at, completed_at, task_count, completed_count, failed_count), JobDetail model (extends JobSummary with events list and tasks list). In factory/dashboard/db.py: implement async SQLite database class with init_db() to create events table, insert_event(), get_jobs() returning JobSummary list, get_job(job_id) returning JobDetail, get_job_log(job_id) returning Event list. Use aiosqlite. Database file location from DASHBOARD_DB_PATH env var, defaulting to ~/.dark-factory/dashboard.db.
acceptance_criteria: EventType enum covers all 9 event types listed
Event Pydantic model has all required fields with proper types
JobSummary and JobDetail models exist with correct fields
init_db() creates the events table in SQLite with correct schema
insert_event() stores an event and returns it with generated id
get_jobs() returns list of JobSummary aggregated from events
get_job() returns JobDetail with full event history
get_job_log() returns chronological Event list for a job
All functions are async and use aiosqlite
Tests cover all CRUD operations using an in-memory SQLite database
make check passes
make test passes

title: Implement Status API endpoints
description: Build the four REST endpoints in factory/dashboard/routers/events.py. POST /api/v1/events accepts an Event body (without id, which is server-generated), stores it via db layer, returns 201 with the created event. GET /api/v1/jobs returns list of all jobs as JobSummary objects, sorted by most recent first. GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id} returns a single JobDetail or 404. GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}/log returns the event log for a job as a list of Events, or 404 if job doesn't exist. Wire the router into the FastAPI app created in task-1. Add CORS middleware allowing all origins (dashboard will run on a different port). Include lifespan handler that calls init_db() on startup.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/events returns 201 with created event including server-generated id
POST /api/v1/events returns 422 for invalid event data
GET /api/v1/jobs returns list of JobSummary sorted by most recent
GET /api/v1/jobs returns empty list when no jobs exist
GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id} returns JobDetail with events and tasks
GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id} returns 404 for unknown job_id
GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}/log returns chronological event list
GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}/log returns 404 for unknown job_id
CORS middleware is configured
Database is initialized on app startup via lifespan
All endpoints have Pydantic response models
Tests use httpx AsyncClient with the FastAPI app (TestClient)
make check passes
make test passes

title: Integrate event emitter into orchestrator
description: Create factory/dashboard/emitter.py with an async EventEmitter class that posts events to the Status API. The emitter should be optional — if DASHBOARD_URL env var is not set, all emit calls are silent no-ops (the orchestrator must work without a dashboard). Use httpx.AsyncClient for HTTP calls. Add emit helper methods: emit_job_started, emit_job_completed, emit_job_failed, emit_agent_spawned, emit_agent_exited, emit_task_started, emit_task_completed, emit_task_failed, emit_round_result. Each method constructs the proper Event payload and POSTs to DASHBOARD_URL/api/v1/events. Catch and log (but never raise) any errors — dashboard failures must not break the orchestrator. Then integrate into factory/orchestrator.py: create an emitter instance at the start of run_job(), and call the appropriate emit methods at each lifecycle point (job start/end, agent spawn/exit, task start/complete/fail, round result).
acceptance_criteria: factory/dashboard/emitter.py exists with EventEmitter class
EventEmitter is a no-op when DASHBOARD_URL is not set
EventEmitter posts events via httpx to DASHBOARD_URL/api/v1/events
All 9 event types have dedicated emit methods
Errors in event posting are logged but never raised
Orchestrator creates EventEmitter at start of run_job()
Events emitted at: job started, job completed, job failed
Events emitted at: task started, task completed, task failed
Events emitted at: each round result (pass/fail)
httpx added to pyproject.toml dependencies
Tests verify emitter sends correct payloads (mock httpx)
Tests verify emitter is silent no-op without DASHBOARD_URL
Tests verify emitter swallows exceptions
make check passes
make test passes

title: Set up React frontend with Tailwind and dark theme
description: Initialize a React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS project in dashboard/frontend/ using Vite. Configure Tailwind with dark mode as default. Set up a proxy to the FastAPI backend (localhost:8420) for /api/ routes in vite.config.ts. Create the app shell: App.tsx with a dark-themed layout, header with 'Dark Factory — Mission Control' title, and placeholder sections for agent cards, task progress, live log, and job history. Add npm scripts: dev, build, preview. Add a Makefile target 'frontend-dev' to run the Vite dev server and 'frontend-build' to produce a production build. Create a types.ts file with TypeScript interfaces matching the API models (Event, JobSummary, JobDetail). Create an api.ts file with fetch functions for all four API endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: dashboard/frontend/ contains a Vite + React + TypeScript project
Tailwind CSS is configured with dark mode as default
vite.config.ts proxies /api/ to localhost:8420
App.tsx renders a dark-themed layout with header and section placeholders
types.ts has TypeScript interfaces for Event, EventType, JobSummary, JobDetail
api.ts has functions: postEvent, getJobs, getJob, getJobLog
npm run dev starts the dev server
npm run build produces a production build without errors
Makefile has 'frontend-dev' and 'frontend-build' targets
package.json includes react, react-dom, tailwindcss, typescript, vite

title: Build dashboard UI components
description: Implement the four main dashboard components in dashboard/frontend/src/components/. AgentCards.tsx: shows cards for Architect, QA Engineer, Developer with status (idle/active/done) derived from latest events, using color-coded borders (gray=idle, blue=active, green=done). TaskProgress.tsx: lists tasks for the selected job with red/green round indicators, shows acceptance criteria completion status. LiveLog.tsx: scrolling event feed that polls GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}/log every 3 seconds, auto-scrolls to bottom, shows timestamp + event type + details with appropriate colors. JobHistory.tsx: table of recent jobs from GET /api/v1/jobs with status badge, duration, repo name, and click to select. Wire all components into App.tsx with state management: selected job ID, auto-refresh via polling. Use Tailwind utility classes throughout, matching a dark industrial theme.
acceptance_criteria: AgentCards component shows Architect, QA, Developer cards with dynamic status
Agent card borders are color-coded: gray=idle, blue=active, green=done
TaskProgress component lists tasks with red/green round indicators
LiveLog component polls every 3 seconds and auto-scrolls
LiveLog entries show timestamp, event type, and details with color coding
JobHistory component shows recent jobs in a table with status badges
Clicking a job in JobHistory selects it and updates other components
All components use Tailwind CSS with dark theme classes
App.tsx manages selected job state and passes it to child components
npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

title: End-to-end integration test
description: Write an integration test that verifies the full pipeline: Status API receives events, stores them correctly, and serves them back through all endpoints. Test in tests/test_dashboard/test_integration.py. The test should: (1) start the FastAPI app using httpx AsyncClient, (2) post a sequence of events simulating a real job lifecycle (job_started, agent_spawned for Architect, task_started, round_result fail, round_result pass, task_completed, agent_exited, job_completed), (3) verify GET /api/v1/jobs returns the job with correct summary stats, (4) verify GET /api/v1/jobs/{id} returns full detail with all events, (5) verify GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}/log returns events in chronological order, (6) verify the frontend builds successfully (npm run build). Also verify that the EventEmitter correctly formats and sends events by integration-testing it against the real API (not mocks).
acceptance_criteria: Integration test simulates a full job lifecycle with 8+ events
GET /api/v1/jobs returns the job with correct task_count, completed_count, status
GET /api/v1/jobs/{id} returns all events in the detail response
GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}/log returns events in chronological order
EventEmitter integration test posts events to real FastAPI app and verifies storage
Frontend production build succeeds (npm run build exits 0)
All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
make check passes
make test passes
